visionDescription: Basierend auf der Analyse der verschiedenen Herausforderungen und Fragestellungen, finden wir innovative Antworten, mit denen Dar-in e.V. zu den führenden interkulturellen Akteuren wird.
missionDescription: Unterschiedliche Kulturen bergen Chancen und Konflikte. Wir schaffen eine Plattform um sie zu identifizieren und analysieren. Mit Ideen und Aktivitäten tragen wir dazu bei, neue Handlungsmöglichkeiten und nachhaltige Lösungen zu finden und womöglich umzusetzen.
valuesDescription: Überwindung von Ignoranz durch: Forschung und Austausch - Innovationsbereitschaft - Ehrlichkeit - Friedlicher Umgang miteinander und Förderung von Vielfalt.

Author: Author
faq_subtitle: Frequently asked questions about the Dar-in e.V. library borrowing rules.
faq_q1: Who is entitled to borrow books?
faq_a1: Association members and registered users may use the borrowing service in accordance with the approved borrowing rules. The administration may request proof of identity or membership when borrowing.
faq_q2: What types of borrowing are available and what do they cost?
faq_a2:
Borrowing itself is free; the borrower only pays postal shipping costs (if any).

Book Companion: 2 books every 2 months (6 shipments per year, 12 books/year). Subscription: 20 EUR per year (covers shipping).

Book Friend: 2 books per month (12 shipments per year, 24 books/year). Subscription: 35 EUR per year (covers shipping).

Family borrowing (remote) and on-site borrowing: conditions on request. On-site borrowing may require a deposit (e.g. 10 EUR per person, max 50 EUR per family), refundable when membership ends and all books are returned in good condition.
faq_q3: What is the maximum number of books that can be borrowed?
faq_a3: It depends on the chosen borrowing scheme. In general, a borrower may have up to six (6) books at a time, unless a specific programme states otherwise.
faq_a4: The loan period is one full month starting from the date of receipt. For postal delivery, shipping time is not counted as part of the loan period.
faq_q5: Can the loan period be extended?
faq_a5: An extension may be requested before the original period ends, provided no one else has reserved the book. The extension only takes effect after approval by the library administration.
faq_a6: Users may reserve books that are currently on loan. They are notified when the book becomes available. The book must be collected within the set period, otherwise the reservation is cancelled automatically.
faq_q7: What happens if books are returned late?
faq_a7: Late returns: late fees apply as per the rules; after 10 days additional fees may apply; after 20 days the association may treat the book as lost and require compensation. Repeated violations may lead to further administrative measures.
faq_q8: What is the borrower's legal responsibility?
faq_a8: The borrower is fully responsible for the book from receipt until return, and must keep it in good condition and not damage it.
faq_q9: What happens if a book is lost or damaged?
faq_a9: In case of loss or serious damage, the borrower must compensate the replacement value or provide an equivalent copy after approval by the administration. Lending borrowed books to anyone other than the registered member is not allowed.
faq_q10: Can a borrowing request be cancelled?
faq_a10: A borrowing request may be cancelled before the books are received. After receipt, the full rules apply.
faq_a11: Yes, provided all books in the borrower's possession are returned before requesting termination. For on-site and family membership, the deposit is refunded after all books are returned in good condition.
faq_q12: Does the administration reserve special rights?
faq_a12: The library administration reserves the right to refuse or restrict borrowing requests in case of violation of the rules, and to make exceptional decisions in special or humanitarian cases in the interest of the association and users.
faq_q13: How is the purchase of books and running of the library financed?
faq_a13: Everyone involved in running the library works on a voluntary basis and is not paid. Book purchases are financed through donations and sometimes through projects supported by the city of Saarbrücken or the Saarland region.
faq_q14: Does the library offer other services?
faq_a14: Yes. The library offers, among other things: advice on choosing suitable books for children and adults; help finding books on specific topics; consideration of reader suggestions when placing new orders; and the regular "Thursday meetup" at the association premises, with the possibility of joining remotely.
faq_q15: Can books be purchased from the library?
faq_a15: The main purpose of the library is to lend books. In exceptional cases (e.g. certain children's books), individual items may be sold at the administration's discretion.
faq_q16: Are all library books available for loan?
faq_a16: Not all books are available for loan. Some reference works, dictionaries and rare books are for on-site use only.
